claim_text: The City of Alvin is considering an Interlocal Agreement with the Rosharon Volunteer Fire Department to provide fire protection services for the Preservation Creek Subdivision Development Area. The agreement would run through December 31, 2026, at a cost not to exceed $12,000.00. This item appears on the April 16, 2026 Consent Agenda as Item 5E.

Council is asked to approve the Interlocal Agreement with the Rosharon Volunteer Fire Department for fire protection services for the Preservation Creek Subdivision Development Area through December 31, 2026, for an amount not to exceed $12,000.00, and to authorize the Mayor to sign the Agreement.

The agreement ensures that the Preservation Creek Subdivision Development Area has formal fire protection coverage, which is a basic public safety requirement for any developing residential area. Authorizing the Mayor to sign formalizes the City's contractual relationship with the Rosharon Volunteer Fire Department for this service. The agreement's end date of December 31, 2026, means council may need to revisit this arrangement in the future.
Amount not to exceed $12,000.00

The City Council is being asked to award a construction bid to Reddico Construction Company, Inc., for the Lift Station 33 Rehabilitation and Expansion Project. The contract value is not to exceed $2,179,995.83. This item appears on the Consent Agenda for the April 16, 2026 regular Council meeting.

source_extracts.text: Consider an award of bid to Reddico Construction Company, Inc., for the Lift Station 33 Rehabilitation and Expansion Project in an amount not to exceed $2,179,995.83; and authorize the City Manager to sign the contract.

claim_text: The Council is being asked to award the bid to Reddico Construction Company, Inc., in an amount not to exceed $2,179,995.83, and to authorize the City Manager to sign the contract.

source_extracts.text: Consider an award of bid to Reddico Construction Company, Inc., for the Lift Station 33 Rehabilitation and Expansion Project in an amount not to exceed $2,179,995.83; and authorize the City Manager to sign the contract.

claim_text: Lift Station 33 is part of the city's wastewater infrastructure, and its rehabilitation and expansion directly affects the reliability of sewer service for Alvin residents. Authorizing the City Manager to sign the contract allows the project to move forward without requiring a separate approval step. As a Consent Agenda item, it is grouped with routine business, signaling staff confidence in the procurement process.

source_extracts.text: Consider an award of bid to Reddico Construction Company, Inc., for the Lift Station 33 Rehabilitation and Expansion Project in an amount not to exceed $2,179,995.83; and authorize the City Manager to sign the contract.

claim_text: Not to exceed $2,179,995.83

source_extracts.text: in an amount not to exceed $2,179,995.83

claim_text: The City of Alvin is considering an amendment to an existing Debris Removal Services Agreement with Crowder Gulf LLC. The purpose of the amendment is to incorporate required federal contract provisions into the agreement. This item appears on the April 16, 2026 Consent Agenda as Item 5G.

source_extracts.text: Consider an amendment to the Debris Removal Services Agreement with Crowder Gulf LLC to incorporate required federal contract provisions.

claim_text: Council is being asked to approve an amendment to the Debris Removal Services Agreement with Crowder Gulf LLC to incorporate required federal contract provisions.

source_extracts.text: Consider an amendment to the Debris Removal Services Agreement with Crowder Gulf LLC to incorporate required federal contract provisions.

claim_text: Incorporating required federal contract provisions is typically necessary to maintain eligibility for federal reimbursement — particularly relevant for debris removal services, which are often activated following disaster events. Failure to include required federal language in such agreements can jeopardize a municipality's ability to recover costs through federal programs. This amendment ensures the City's contract with Crowder Gulf LLC meets those compliance standards.

source_extracts.text: Consider an amendment to the Debris Removal Services Agreement with Crowder Gulf LLC to incorporate required federal contract provisions.

claim_text: The Alvin City Council is being asked to consider Ordinance 26-C, which would release approximately 5.2692 acres of land from the City's Extraterritorial Jurisdiction (ETJ). The ordinance includes findings of fact and a severability clause. This item appears under Other Business (Item 6B) on the April 16, 2026 agenda.

source_extracts.text: Consider Ordinance 26-C, releasing approximately 5.2692 acres of land from the Extraterritorial Jurisdiction of the City; providing findings of fact; containing a severability clause; and providing for other matters related thereto.

claim_text: The council is being asked to vote on whether to adopt Ordinance 26-C, releasing approximately 5.2692 acres of land from the City of Alvin's Extraterritorial Jurisdiction.

Releasing land from the ETJ removes the City's planning and regulatory authority over that acreage, potentially affecting future annexation options and land use control in the affected area. The ordinance's findings of fact will establish the legal basis for the release, which could have implications for the city's growth boundaries and jurisdictional reach.

The Alvin City Council is considering Ordinance 26-D, which would amend Chapter 24, "Traffic and Vehicles," of the City's Code of Ordinances. The ordinance targets two existing articles — Article II (Parking) and Article III (Commercial Motor Vehicles) — to establish new regulations on where and when certain vehicles may be parked, specifically vehicles exceeding twenty-five (25) feet in length and commercial motor vehicles during certain hours. The ordinance also provides definitions relevant to its provisions and includes standard legal clauses for repealer, severability, publication, and effective date.

source_extracts.text: Consider Ordinance 26-D, amending Chapter 24, "Traffic and Vehicles" of the Code of Ordinances by amending Article II (Parking) and Article III (Commercial Motor Vehicles) to regulate the parking of certain vehicles, including vehicles exceeding twenty-five (25) feet in length and commercial motor vehicles during certain hours; providing definitions; providing for a penalty not to exceed five hundred dollars ($500) for violations; providing for repealer; providing for severability; providing for publication; and providing for effective date.

claim_text: The Council is being asked to vote on whether to adopt Ordinance 26-D, amending Chapter 24 of the Code of Ordinances to regulate the parking of vehicles exceeding twenty-five (25) feet in length and commercial motor vehicles during certain hours, with a penalty not to exceed five hundred dollars ($500) for violations.

source_extracts.text: providing for a penalty not to exceed five hundred dollars ($500) for violations; providing for repealer; providing for severability; providing for publication; and providing for effective date.

claim_text: Adoption of this ordinance would create enforceable parking restrictions for large vehicles and commercial motor vehicles within the city, which could affect residents, businesses, and property owners who park or operate such vehicles. Violations would carry a penalty of up to five hundred dollars ($500), making compliance a financial consideration for affected parties. The ordinance represents a formal update to the city's traffic and vehicle code and would have legal effect upon publication.

source_extracts.text: to regulate the parking of certain vehicles, including vehicles exceeding twenty-five (25) feet in length and commercial motor vehicles during certain hours; providing definitions; providing for a penalty not to exceed five hundred dollars ($500) for violations

claim_text: The Alvin City Council is considering Resolution 26-R-20, which would authorize the Alvin Police Department to apply for the Catalytic Converter Theft Prevention Grant administered by the Motor Vehicle Crime Prevention Authority. If awarded, the grant funds would help defray the cost of a Drone First Responder (DFR) system and eight (8) additional FLOCK ALPR cameras from FLOCK Safety.

Council is asked to approve Resolution 26-R-20, authorizing the Alvin Police Department to submit a grant application to the Motor Vehicle Crime Prevention Authority for funding toward a Drone First Responder system and eight additional FLOCK ALPR cameras.

Approval of this resolution allows the Alvin Police Department to pursue outside grant funding to expand its surveillance and rapid-response technology capabilities, specifically targeting catalytic converter theft — a crime affecting vehicle owners across the community. Securing the grant would help offset costs to the city for the DFR system and additional license plate reader cameras, which could enhance the department's ability to detect and respond to motor vehicle crimes.
The agenda text does not specify a total project cost or grant amount; the grant is described as being intended to "help defray the cost" of the listed equipment.

The City of Alvin is considering Ordinance 26-E, which would amend the City's existing Park Rules to add new prohibitions governing conduct within city parks. Specifically, the ordinance would prohibit unauthorized digging, excavation, or ground disturbance, as well as prohibit climbing, standing, or sitting on certain park structures. The ordinance also includes other related provisions.

source_extracts.text: Consider Ordinance 26-E, amending the City of Alvin Park Rules, adding provisions prohibiting unauthorized digging, excavation, or ground disturbance; prohibiting climbing, standing, or sitting on certain park structures within City parks; and providing for other provisions related thereto.

claim_text: The council is being asked to consider and vote on Ordinance 26-E, which would amend the City of Alvin Park Rules to add the new prohibitions described.

source_extracts.text: Consider Ordinance 26-E, amending the City of Alvin Park Rules, adding provisions prohibiting unauthorized digging, excavation, or ground disturbance; prohibiting climbing, standing, or sitting on certain park structures within City parks; and providing for other provisions related thereto.

claim_text: These amendments would establish clearer rules of conduct for park visitors in Alvin, potentially allowing staff and law enforcement to address unsafe or damaging behaviors in city parks. Residents who use city parks would be subject to the new prohibitions, and violations could carry enforcement consequences under the amended ordinance.

source_extracts.text: amending the City of Alvin Park Rules, adding provisions prohibiting unauthorized digging, excavation, or ground disturbance; prohibiting climbing, standing, or sitting on certain park structures within City parks

claim_text: The Alvin City Council is being asked to consider Resolution 26-R-21, which would amend the Rules and Regulations for Bob S. Owen Pool and Facilities, located at 919 Bayou Drive, Alvin, Texas. This item appears on the April 16, 2026, Regular Meeting agenda as Item 6F under Other Business. The agenda does not provide additional detail on the specific changes being proposed to the rules and regulations.

source_extracts.text: Consider Resolution 26-R-21, amending the Rules and Regulations for Bob S. Owen Pool and Facilities, located at 919 Bayou Drive, Alvin, Texas; and setting forth other matters related thereto.

claim_text: Council is asked to vote on whether to approve Resolution 26-R-21, amending the Rules and Regulations for Bob S. Owen Pool and Facilities at 919 Bayou Drive, Alvin, Texas.

source_extracts.text: Consider Resolution 26-R-21, amending the Rules and Regulations for Bob S. Owen Pool and Facilities, located at 919 Bayou Drive, Alvin, Texas; and setting forth other matters related thereto.

claim_text: Bob S. Owen Pool and Facilities is a City of Alvin public amenity, and changes to its rules and regulations would directly govern how residents and visitors use that facility. This item is one of two pool-related resolutions on the same agenda — the other (Resolution 26-R-22) addresses the Alvin Recreation Center Pool — suggesting a potential coordinated update to aquatic facility policies across the city.

source_extracts.text: Consider Resolution 26-R-22, amending the Rules and Regulations of the Alvin Recreation Center Pool and Facilities; located at 3201 Highway 35, Alvin, Texas; and setting forth other matters related thereto.

claim_text: The Alvin City Council is being asked to consider Resolution 26-R-22, which would amend the Rules and Regulations governing the Alvin Recreation Center Pool and Facilities located at 3201 Highway 35, Alvin, Texas. This item appears on the April 16, 2026 regular meeting agenda as item 6G under Other Business. A companion item, Resolution 26-R-21, addresses the Rules and Regulations for a separate facility, Bob S. Owen Pool at 919 Bayou Drive, and appears immediately preceding this item as 6F.

source_extracts.text: Consider Resolution 26-R-22, amending the Rules and Regulations of the Alvin Recreation Center Pool and Facilities; located at 3201 Highway 35, Alvin, Texas; and setting forth other matters related thereto.

claim_text: The council is being asked to vote on whether to approve Resolution 26-R-22 amending the Rules and Regulations of the Alvin Recreation Center Pool and Facilities located at 3201 Highway 35, Alvin, Texas.

source_extracts.text: Consider Resolution 26-R-22, amending the Rules and Regulations of the Alvin Recreation Center Pool and Facilities; located at 3201 Highway 35, Alvin, Texas; and setting forth other matters related thereto.

claim_text: The amended rules and regulations would govern conduct and use of the Alvin Recreation Center Pool and Facilities, directly affecting residents who use that facility. As a formal resolution, the amended rules would carry official city policy weight. The council's action on this item, alongside the companion Resolution 26-R-21 for Bob S. Owen Pool, suggests a broader review of aquatic facility policies across the city.
